nuggets.headline: A teenage Baby Keem was a key collaborator on "DAMN."
nuggets.text: During the 2016 recording sessions for "DAMN.", Kendrick Lamar brought his cousin, a then-rising Baby Keem, into the studio. Keem's "fresh, innovative energy" and knack for hooks complemented Lamar's introspective lyricism, and this familial synergy was a significant part of the album's creative process. Their work together during this period laid the foundation for their future, more prominent collaborations.

source.title: Inside the Studio: Kendrick Lamar & Baby Keem Recording Music for “DAMN.” in 2016 – This Is Hip Hop HQ Magazine
source.publisher: This Is Hip Hop HQ Magazine
source.quoteSnippet: The synergy between Lamar and Baby Keem was palpable. Lamar’s narrative-driven approach to songwriting meshed seamlessly with Baby Keem’s knack for crafting catchy hooks and innovative beats.

nuggets.imageCaption: The DAMN. sessions were characterized by intense, hands-on collaboration in the studio.

nuggets.headline: The beat for "HUMBLE." was originally made for Gucci Mane
nuggets.text: Producer Mike Will Made It initially created this beat with Gucci Mane in mind. After Kendrick Lamar recorded his vocals, the track was slated for Mike Will's own album, "Ransom 2". It was only after others convinced Lamar to keep it that "HUMBLE." became the lead single and the very first song recorded for the album "DAMN.".

source.title: List of awards and nominations received by Kendrick Lamar
source.quoteSnippet: The beat for "Humble" was developed by Mike Will Made It, with the intention of recording the song with Gucci Mane... After recording, it was initially agreed upon that it would be released on Mike Will Made's debut album Ransom 2.

nuggets.imageCaption: A still from the award-winning music video for "HUMBLE."

nuggets.headline: To hear the roots of Kendrick Lamar's TDE crew, listen to ScHoolboy Q
nuggets.text: Before their solo careers took off, Kendrick Lamar and ScHoolboy Q formed the core of the supergroup Black Hippy, alongside Jay Rock and Ab-Soul. They all developed their sounds on the Top Dawg Entertainment (TDE) label, and their early work showcases the chemistry that defined a new era of West Coast hip-hop. For a prime example of that TDE energy, start with ScHoolboy Q's album "Oxymoron".

source.title: The 50 Greatest Kendrick Lamar Songs
source.quoteSnippet: Kendrick’s breakthrough mixtape, Overly Dedicated, pulls beats from midperiod Roots albums... 'Michael Jordan' is a lone concession to the maximalist streak that ruled rap production circa 2010, Kendrick and ScHoolboy Q’s playfully clipped vocals dancing on top of Sounwave’s massive beat.

nuggets.imageCaption: ScHoolboy Q, Kendrick Lamar's label-mate and collaborator in Black Hippy.

artistSummary: Kendrick Lamar is a Pulitzer Prize-winning rapper from Compton, California, known for his dense lyrical narratives and genre-bending production. His work consistently pushes the boundaries of hip-hop, exploring themes of race, identity, and spirituality with unflinching honesty [CIT 14].
